method_atom: A phase-space representation of a many-body system that enforces a conserved total quantity, built on generalized group- (SU(M)-type) coherent states, which reduces in the macroscopic large-population limit to a deterministic Liouvillian flow that can be sampled efficiently by Monte Carlo methods. By tracking higher statistical moments rather than a single mean configuration, it captures collective phenomena such as dynamical instabilities and chaotic dynamics that a mean-field approximation misses, and it demonstrates that imposing the exact conservation constraint eliminates the spurious fluctuations and ambiguities that a non-conserving approximation would introduce.
object_atom: Interacting bosons hopping among a small number of modes or lattice sites (the two- and three-mode Bose-Hubbard model), including an open-quantum-system variant, with particular attention to the depletion and heating of a Bose-Einstein condensate.
brief_reason: The constraint-conserving coherent-state phase-space / Liouvillian-flow machinery that goes beyond a mean-field limit is the transferable method, while the few-mode Bose-Hubbard system and its condensate are the physics-specific object.
